tagline: Licensed commercial roofing for LA County businesses and property managers.
metaDescription: Commercial roofing contractor for Los Angeles County — TPO, modified bitumen, built-up, SPF foam, and metal systems. Free inspections. CA Lic. #1109337.
intro_h2: Commercial Roofing Across Los Angeles County
intro: Palm Crest Roofing serves commercial property owners, managers, and tenants across LA County with full-service commercial roofing. From small retail buildings to large warehouses and multi-tenant HOAs, we handle re-roofs, recovers, repairs, and preventative maintenance on every major low-slope system in use today.
benefits: TPO, PVC, modified bitumen, built-up, and SPF foam systems
Minimal business disruption — flexible scheduling including nights/weekends
Coordination with property managers, HOAs, and tenants
Detailed written scopes for budget planning and RFPs

process.title: Site Survey
process.desc: We inspect the roof, document existing conditions, identify drains and penetrations, and take core samples if needed.

process.title: Proposal
process.desc: Detailed scope and pricing — broken out by system, warranty, and options so you can compare apples-to-apples.

process.title: Pre-Construction Meeting
process.desc: Coordinate logistics with your facilities team: access, parking, noise, safety, and tenant communication.

process.title: Installation
process.desc: Licensed crew, proper fall protection, material loading plan, and daily site cleanup.

process.title: Close-Out
process.desc: Final inspection, as-built documentation, and manufacturer warranty registration.

faqs.q: What commercial roofing systems do you install?
faqs.a: TPO, PVC, modified bitumen (torch-down and cold-applied), built-up, SPF foam, and standing-seam metal. We'll recommend the right system based on your building type, budget, and local climate.
Yes. We offer annual and semi-annual maintenance contracts that extend roof life and catch issues before they become leaks.

faqs.q: Can you work around our business hours?
faqs.a: Absolutely. We regularly work nights, weekends, and phased installs to minimize disruption to tenants and operations.
